author: Dr. Lena Park (MICU attending) / Dr. Daniel Okafor (Medicine)
content:
MICU ADMISSION H&P + COURSE — UNIVERSITY MEDICAL CENTER
Date: 4/28/2027 (course addended through discharge 4/30)

HPI: 50F with decompensated cirrhosis who has been receiving WEEKLY outpatient thoracentesis and paracentesis at a community facility for several weeks. She presented to the ED with acute shortness of breath. On arrival: afebrile, BP 104/65, HR 112, RR 30, SpO2 82% — using accessory muscles, fatigued, in respiratory distress. CXR (IMG003): diffuse BILATERAL pulmonary edema (distinct from her chronic isolated right transudative effusion).

COURSE:
- Intubated for hypoxemic respiratory failure; admitted to MICU.
- Workup: BNP markedly elevated (920); troponin negative; afebrile with normal WBC; bedside echo (IMG004) with preserved EF and no valvular/regional abnormality. The picture is acute VOLUME OVERLOAD rather than infection, ischemia, or new structural heart disease.
- Treated with IV furosemide with brisk diuresis and rapid improvement in oxygenation.
- Extubated on 4/29 (the next day). Weaned to nasal cannula, then room air.
- Discharged home 4/30 at her respiratory baseline.

ASSESSMENT: Flash pulmonary edema. Reviewing her recent course, she has been receiving 4 units of FFP plus albumin at EACH weekly outpatient drainage session (for an elevated INR) — a substantial repeated volume/oncotic load in a patient with limited capacity to handle it. The most likely cause of her flash pulmonary edema is this repeated peri-procedural FFP administration (transfusion-associated circulatory overload). She has not tolerated diuretics and plans to resume her weekly procedures with the same outside radiologist.

PLAN:
- Communicate with the outpatient radiologist regarding the peri-procedural transfusion practice.
- Hepatology and IR to advise on a safer longer-term drainage strategy.
- Continue transplant evaluation.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Lena Park, MD (MICU) and Dr. Daniel Okafor, MD (Medicine). 4/30/2027.
